channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: West Vancouver police stop impaired learner driver behind the wheel of Ferrari
item.description: West Vancouver police say a 20-year-old driver with a learner’s ‘L’ licence was stopped after being found operating a Ferrari while impaired. The incident occurred in West Vancouver, British Columbia,...

item.title: David Bowie’s Iconic Role as Jareth in Jim Henson’s Labyrinth (1986)
item.description: The article revisits David Bowie’s unforgettable portrayal of Jareth the Goblin King in Jim Henson’s 1986 fantasy film Labyrinth, a cult classic that has remained influential for decades. In the film,...

item.title: Tanroads clarifies redevelopment works at former Kimara demolition sites amid public concerns
item.description: In Kimara area of Dar es Salaam, new construction works taking place on sites where earlier demolitions were carried out have sparked public discussion among residents and stakeholders. The Tanzania N...

item.title: India’s forex reserves decline by $8.9 billion amid fall in foreign currency and gold holdings
item.description: India’s foreign exchange reserves recorded a decline of $8.9 billion, bringing the total to $688.8 billion for the week ending May 15, according to data released by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). Th...

item.title: Nigerian Navy shuts illegal refined petroleum storage facility in Okrika, Rivers State
item.description: The Nigerian Navy has successfully dismantled an illegal refined petroleum products depot located in Okrika, Rivers State, as part of its ongoing crackdown on crude oil theft and illicit petroleum act...

item.title: Kenya reports steady progress toward 15 billion tree planting and ecosystem restoration goals by 2032
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has confirmed that Kenya is steadily moving towards its ambitious target of planting 15 billion trees by the year 2032 as part of the country’s broader climate change ...

item.title: DRC keeps World Cup training plans despite US Ebola-related travel warning
item.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) national football team says it will continue with its planned preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup despite a warning from United States authorities linke...

item.title: Hajj arrivals exceed last year’s international pilgrims despite regional conflict
item.description: According to a Saudi official, more than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia from outside the kingdom for this year’s Hajj season, even though the Middle East has been affected by ongoin...

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo may impact World Cup plans and reopen qualification chance for Nigeria
item.description: The qualification race for the 2026 FIFA World Cup has taken a new twist following a serious Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), which may indirectly affect Nigeria’s Super Eagle...

item.title: A humorous look at different styles of people packing for vacations
item.description: This opinion piece by illustrator Edith Pritchett offers a lighthearted and humorous take on the different kinds of people when it comes to packing for vacations. Instead of focusing on travel destina...

item.title: Damaged Keni Bridge in Bihar’s Gaya Raises Safety Concerns After Structural Cracks and Exposed Rods Found
item.description: The condition of the Keni bridge in Bihar’s Gaya district has triggered serious concern among local residents and authorities after visuals showing major structural damage surfaced online. The bridge,...

item.title: Conrad Black argues Alberta separatism reflects broader tensions over energy policy, Indigenous rights and federal governance
item.description: In this opinion column, Conrad Black argues that growing frustration in Alberta over federal energy policies and legal disputes surrounding pipelines could strengthen support for Alberta separatism. H...

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard to step down as US Director of National Intelligence amid husband’s cancer diagnosis
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard has announced she will resign from her role as the United States Director of National Intelligence, with her departure set to take effect on 30 June. Gabbard said her decision is driven ...

item.title: MARU Japanese restaurant opens at Africa House Garden, boosting Zanzibar’s dining and tourism scene
item.description: Zanzibar’s tourism and hospitality sector has received a fresh boost with the opening of MARU Restaurant, the island’s first dedicated Japanese dining spot, located at the newly completed Africa House...

item.title: Shirley Bassey’s Early Career and Rise to Fame in the 1950s
item.description: This article explores the early life and career of Welsh singer Shirley Bassey during the 1950s, a formative decade that shaped her path to international stardom. Born in Cardiff in 1937, Bassey left ...

item.title: A Rare Brain Condition Led to Emergency Surgery After Severe Headaches and Misdiagnosed Symptoms
item.description: The article is a first-person account by a 64-year-old woman who experienced a sudden and severe medical crisis following months of unexplained headaches. Initially, she developed persistent head pain...

item.title: FBI Seeks Expanded Real-Time Access to Nationwide License Plate Reader Data Amid Broader US Cybersecurity and Privacy Concerns
item.description: WIRED’s weekly security roundup focused heavily on surveillance, cybersecurity vulnerabilities, online abuse, and government oversight in the United States. The main story revealed that the FBI is pre...

item.title: CRS report outlines major US aircraft losses and operational costs during 40-day Iran conflict
item.description: A report prepared by the US Congressional Research Service (CRS) has provided one of the most detailed public accounts so far of American aircraft losses during Operation Epic Fury, the 40-day militar...

item.title: Khawaja Asif says Pakistan’s mediation efforts between the US and Iran are moving towards possible progress
item.description: Defence Minister Khawaja Asif has said that Pakistan’s diplomatic efforts to mediate between the United States and Iran are gradually producing encouraging signs. Speaking to Geo News in Sialkot, Asif...

item.title: Kamloops residential school claims spark debate over free speech and censorship in Canada
item.description: This opinion piece from the National Post examines the controversy surrounding public discourse on residential schools following the 2021 announcement by Tk’emlúps te Secwépemc First Nation regarding ...
